title: Web demo-only scope statement
summary: Write and verify the M7 Web demo-only scope statement from M6 evidence without claiming same-spec, lower-fast-forward or full release support.
acceptance: States Web demo scope, limitations, performance/storage expectations and player-facing boundaries from verified M6 evidence
Ensures Web is not described as same-spec, lower-fast-forward, lower-cap or full release support
Keeps Web OPFS/import/export, local diagnostics and SharedArrayBuffer fallback claims grounded in M6 evidence
Provides citable language for downstream store/playtest materials and known issues
Does not cancel Web, launch Web publicly or change the M6 Web verdict

history.details: Created by WM-0098 M7 planning DAG; remains proposed until WM-0098 is verified, integrated and done.

Added M7 Web demo-only scope statement and Web release-gate note grounded in reviewed M6 evidence; safe downstream copy preserves demo-only verdict, blocks same-spec/lower-fast-forward/lower-cap/public launch claims, and keeps owner gates; required checks passed including build:web, web-shell e2e and quality.

verified: No blocking findings; demo-only Web scope is grounded in verified M6 evidence, blocks same-spec/lower-fast-forward/lower-cap/public-launch claims, preserves owner gates, and required check evidence was reproduced.

Integrated verified Web demo-only scope after independent reviewer confirmed no overclaim, no verdict change and no public launch/release work. Integration gates passed: validate-handoff, taskctl validate/status, git diff --check and pnpm quality.

objective: Make Web external-test messaging truthful and bounded by the M6 demo-only verdict.
forbiddenPaths: same-spec Web claim without new reviewed browser authority evidence
public Web launch or release upload
telemetry accounts paid services crash upload or public feedback system
final privacy legal store or save-compatibility claims
M8 task creation, promotion, claim, implementation or review
risksAndFileOwnership: Risk: Web copy can overclaim support tier; preserve exact demo-only language.
Risk: Web scope docs can become public launch approval; keep release actions owner-gated.
File ownership is limited to Web scope docs/shell surfaces and WM-0104 reports.
ownerGate: Block if any work claims same-spec, lower-fast-forward or full Web support without new reviewed evidence
Block on public launch, store submission, telemetry, accounts, paid services or final public save promises
Block if Web verdict would be changed or Web cancelled without owner approval
m0M6RegressionCoverage: Run Web build and web-shell smoke
Preserve M5 hashes and M6 Web storage/diagnostics evidence boundaries
benchmarkImpact: No benchmark baseline update. Any new Web performance measurement must cite existing M6 threshold policy and not weaken it.
reviewRouting: client-engineer completes to reviewer; reviewer verifies scope statement accuracy, M6 evidence citations and no public Web launch work.
rollbackModel: Revert Web scope docs/shell copy and WM-0104 reports; leave M6 verdicts intact.

sparkEligibility.reason: Web demo-only scope is a product verdict and public-facing boundary task.

review.summary: No blocking findings; demo-only Web scope is grounded in verified M6 evidence, blocks same-spec/lower-fast-forward/lower-cap/public-launch claims, preserves owner gates, and required check evidence was reproduced.

integration.summary: Integrated verified Web demo-only scope after independent reviewer confirmed no overclaim, no verdict change and no public launch/release work. Integration gates passed: validate-handoff, taskctl validate/status, git diff --check and pnpm quality.
